title: KChat Guardrail Encoder Classifier Output
description: Constrained JSON output schema emitted by the on-device encoder classifier. The runtime rejects any output that does not validate against this schema. See ARCHITECTURE.md 'Output Schema'.

severity.description: Severity level per the 0-5 severity rubric.

rationale_id.description: Closed-phrasebook id mapping to a public rationale phrase. No free text.

resource_link_id.description: Localized resource link id (e.g. crisis hotline, electoral authority). The runtime resolves the URL locally; the classifier never emits a resolved URL.

model_health.description: Safety-relevant status signal describing whether the encoder model produced this verdict (``healthy``) or whether the pipeline fell back to a degraded mode in which only the deterministic detectors fired (``model_unavailable`` when the encoder could not run; ``inference_error`` when it ran but emitted output that did not validate against this schema). Absence of the field is equivalent to ``healthy``. This is not message content or an embedding — it is a coarse health signal so the UI can distinguish 'safe message' from 'model couldn't run'. **Consumers MUST inspect this field**: when the value is anything other than ``healthy``, the deterministic detectors are the only signal that ran, so reason codes such as ``PRIVATE_DATA_PATTERN`` / ``SCAM_PATTERN`` / ``URL_RISK`` / ``LEXICON_HIT`` may be present on a ``category=0`` (SAFE) verdict and should be surfaced to the end user. A UI that only inspects ``severity`` / ``actions`` will silently miss harm signals in degraded mode.
